title: Percentage types
description: Percentage and basis-point representations used for rates, fees, ownership stakes, and other proportional values

Percentage.description: A proportional value expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1, where 1 = 100%. Used for management fees, carried interest rates, ownership percentages, discount rates, and LP allocation shares. The maximum of 1 is a soft constraint — values like 1.5 (150%) are valid in some contexts (e.g., overcollateralization ratios) but should be exceptional. Examples: 0.02 = 2% management fee, 0.20 = 20% carried interest, 0.0825 = 8.25% preferred return

BasisPoints.description: A rate expressed in basis points, where 100 bps = 1% and 10000 bps = 100%. Used for spreads, margins, and fee rates in credit facilities where basis-point precision is standard market convention. Integer type avoids floating-point issues. Examples: 250 = 2.50% (SOFR + 250bps), 50 = 0.50% (commitment fee), 15 = 0.15% (agency fee)
